{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48722", "cvss": {"score": 5.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-276", "name": "Incorrect Default Permissions"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-732", "name": "Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource"}], "description": "### Impact

`nextflow auth login` persists Seqera Platform OIDC tokens to `${NXF_HOME:-~/.nextflow}/seqera-auth.config`. The file is created via Java NIO without specifying file permissions, so under the default `umask 022` it lands at mode `0644` (world-readable).

On a multi-user POSIX host — typically an HPC login node, shared workstation, or jump host — any local user able to traverse the victim's home directory can read the file and obtain a valid Platform bearer token, enabling impersonation against Seqera Platform within the token's scope.

Single-user systems and headless CI runners, which do not invoke the interactive login flow, are not affected.

Affected versions:  `25.09.2-edge` through `26.04.1`.
  
### Patches

Fixed in `<PATCHED_VERSION>`. The patched code applies mode `0600` to `seqera-auth.config` immediately after writing it, and re-applies on every subsequent login so any pre-existing world-readable copy left by an earlier version is tightened.

Tokens previously stored in the file must be treated as disclosed. After upgrading, run `nextflow auth logout`, revoke the token in the Seqera Platform UI, and run `nextflow auth login` again.

### Workarounds
  
Restrict the file and its parent directory:

`chmod 600 "${NXF_HOME:-$HOME/.nextflow}/seqera-auth.config"`
`chmod 700 "${NXF_HOME:-$HOME/.nextflow}"`

Alternatively, supply the Platform token via the `TOWER_ACCESS_TOKEN` environment variable instead of running `nextflow auth login`.

### References
  
  - https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/276.html", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-25T21:45:55Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48722"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-25T21:45:55Z", "references": ["https://github.com/nextflow-io/nextflow/security/advisories/GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/nextflow-io/nextflow/security-advisories/GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/nextflow-io/nextflow", "summary": "nextflow auth login command has incorrect default permissions", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-25T21:45:56Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-92qf-fcph-v5wr", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "25.10.6", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.nextflow:nextflow"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 25.09.2-edge, < 25.10.6"}, {"first_patched_version": "26.04.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.nextflow:nextflow"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 26.00.0-edge, < 26.04.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: nextflow auth login command has incorrect default permissions
